key_themes: Recent insider buying by Chairman and directors (multiple PDMR transactions in April 2026, including ~£39k by Peter Harrison)
Five-fold increase in Tau/neuro royalties to £150k driven by Alzheimer's disease research assays
High dividend yield (~8%) with interim dividend maintained at 70p despite modest H1 revenue/PBT decline
Stable core antibody business, strong cash position (£5.3m), and full-year trading in line with expectations
Mixed longer-term shareholder returns and stock pullback from highs, offset by institutional ownership and high margins
summary: Bioventix, a UK developer of high-affinity monoclonal antibodies for clinical diagnostics, shows positive sentiment driven by repeated director share purchases in April 2026 and strong growth in its neuro/Tau royalty stream linked to Alzheimer's applications. H1 2026 results (ended Dec 2025) showed a modest revenue decline to £6.16m and PBT of £4.85m due to FX effects, but constant-currency figures were stable-to-up, cash rose slightly, the interim dividend was held, and the company expressed confidence in meeting full-year expectations. With robust profitability, an attractive ~8% yield, and insider buying signals, the stock appeals to income and long-term investors despite its retreat from 52-week highs near £30.
sources: 10+ news/articles (Yahoo Finance, LSE/RNS, company announcements, Simply Wall St, MarketBeat), ~5-8 relevant recent X posts (primarily director buy mentions and small-cap discussions)
